root_text.sn55.39:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme.
root_text.sn55.39:1.2: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena kāḷigodhāya sākiyāniyā nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.sn55.39:1.3: Atha kho kāḷigodhā sākiyānī yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho kāḷigodhaṁ sākiyāniṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.39:2.1: “Catūhi kho, godhe, dhammehi samannāgatā ariyasāvikā sotāpannā hoti avinipātadhammā niyatā sambodhiparāyaṇā.
root_text.sn55.39:2.3: Idha, godhe, ariyasāvikā buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgatā hoti—
root_text.sn55.39:2.4: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.39:2.7: vigatamalamaccherena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati muttacāgā payatapāṇinī vossaggaratā yācayogā dānasaṁvibhāgaratā.
root_text.sn55.39:2.8: Imehi kho, godhe, catūhi dhammehi samannāgatā ariyasāvikā sotāpannā hoti avinipātadhammā niyatā sambodhiparāyaṇā”ti.
root_text.sn55.39:3.1: “Yānimāni, bhante, bhagavatā cattāri sotāpattiyaṅgāni desitāni, saṁvijjante dhammā mayi, ahañca tesu dhammesu sandissāmi.
root_text.sn55.39:3.3: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.39:3.6: yaṁ kho pana kiñci kule deyyadhammaṁ sabbaṁ taṁ appaṭivibhattaṁ sīlavantehi kalyāṇadhammehī”ti.

translation_text.sn55.39:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sakyans, near Kapilavatthu in the Banyan Tree Monastery.
translation_text.sn55.39:1.2: Then the Buddha robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, went to the home of Kāḷigodhā the Sakyan lady, where he sat on the seat spread out.
translation_text.sn55.39:1.3: Then Kāḷigodhā went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to her:
translation_text.sn55.39:2.1: “Godhā, a female noble disciple who has four things is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.
translation_text.sn55.39:2.3: It’s when a female noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.39:2.7: And she lives at home rid of the stain of stinginess, freely generous, open-handed, loving to let go, committed to charity, loving to give and to share.
translation_text.sn55.39:2.8: A female noble disciple who has these four things is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.”
translation_text.sn55.39:3.1: “Sir, these four factors of stream-entry that were taught by the Buddha are found in me, and I exhibit them.
translation_text.sn55.39:3.2: For I have experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.39:3.6: And I share without reservation all the gifts available to give in our family with those who are ethical and of good character.”
translation_text.sn55.39:3.8: You have declared the fruit of stream-entry.”

variant_text.sn55.39:2.7: payatapāṇinī → payatapāṇī (bj, cck, sya1ed, pts1ed); payatapāṇi (sya2ed)
